analyses.summary: CSX is my cleanest breakout today — a rail leader pushing a new high without being overbought, backed by real earnings growth.

fundamentals.summary: 26% EPS growth with steady margins; rails are a quality, cash-generative business.

technical.summary: Breaking out 0.3% from the 52-week high with RSI 58.9 — momentum confirmed but not extended. The ideal entry profile.

earnings.summary: Recent prints beat; next report not imminent, so low binary risk right now.
signals: No earnings within the entry window

analyst.summary: Consensus skews buy with rising estimates into the freight upcycle.

valuation.summary: Reasonable for a rail at this growth rate — not cheap, not stretched.
signals: Forward P/E in line with rail peers

insider.summary: No notable recent insider selling; nothing flashing on either side.

risk.summary: Rails are economically sensitive — freight-volume softness is the main risk. R:R ~2.5:1 is below my 3:1 ideal, so size accordingly.

catalyst.summary: Broadening industrial leadership and firm freight data support continuation.

analyses.summary: Best fundamentals of today's leaders, but extended at the high — I want a pullback before committing.

fundamentals.summary: 33% EPS growth as a primary AI-capex beneficiary; the structural story is strong.

technical.summary: At new highs but RSI 74.7 — overbought. Chasing here risks a shakeout; wait for a tight pullback to the pivot.

analyst.summary: Broadly bullish coverage on the capex cycle.

valuation.summary: Premium multiple reflecting the growth — leaves less margin for error up here.

risk.summary: Overbought entry + cyclical semis = elevated near-term pullback risk. That's why this is WATCH, not BUY.

catalyst.summary: Rising 2026 capex estimates keep the demand backdrop favorable.
